 {"id":"phage_explorer-2yf3","title":"Reposition FAB to avoid swipe indicator collision","description":"## Problem

The FAB (Floating Action Button) is positioned at `bottom: 16px; right: 16px` which overlaps with swipe navigation indicators in the lower-right area. This creates visual confusion and obscures the swipe affordance.

## Current State (packages/web/src/styles/fab.css)

```css
.fab {
  position: fixed;
  bottom: calc(16px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px));
  right: calc(16px + env(safe-area-inset-right, 0px));
  width: 56px;
  height: 56px;
  z-index: var(--z-fixed); /* 300 */
}
```

The ControlDeck is also at z-index --z-fixed with height 56px + safe-area-inset-bottom.

## Solution Options Considered

### Option A: Move FAB above ControlDeck (RECOMMENDED)
Position FAB above the bottom navigation bar:
```css
.fab {
  bottom: calc(72px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px)); /* 56px deck + 16px gap */
}
```
**Pros**: FAB stays accessible, creates clear visual hierarchy
**Cons**: Higher on screen takes more thumb reach

### Option B: Hide FAB when swipe indicators show
Animate FAB out when swipe hints are visible.
**Pros**: Maximum clarity
**Cons**: Complex state management, FAB feels unreliable

### Option C: Move FAB to left side
Position at bottom-left instead.
**Pros**: Avoids right-side indicators
**Cons**: Non-standard FAB position, could conflict with other UI

## Implementation Plan (Option A)

1. Update `packages/web/src/styles/fab.css`:
   - Change bottom positioning to sit above ControlDeck
   - Add mobile media query for conditional positioning

2. Consider landscape adjustments:
   - ControlDeck is 48px in landscape
   - FAB should adjust accordingly

3. Test on various device sizes and safe-area scenarios

## Files to Modify

- `packages/web/src/styles/fab.css`

## Acceptance Criteria

- FAB does not overlap with any content in the bottom-right corner
- FAB remains easily thumb-reachable on typical phone screens
- FAB position accounts for safe-area-inset-bottom on notched devices
- FAB looks correct in both portrait and landscape orientations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T17:35:21.630294698Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:45:30.771901511Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:45:30.771901511Z","close_reason":"Implemented FAB repositioning above ControlDeck with portrait (72px) and landscape (64px) adjustments","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-2yf3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-355n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T17:35:21.636501114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-355n","title":"Epic: Mobile Phage Navigation UX Polish","description":"## Background & Problem Statement

The mobile webapp has several UX issues around phage navigation discoverability:

1. **FAB Occlusion**: The Floating Action Button (56x56px) positioned at `bottom: 16px; right: 16px` overlaps with swipe gesture indicators, making them invisible or confusing.

2. **Hidden Swipe Affordance**: The app supports horizontal swipe gestures to navigate between phages (implemented in App.tsx:440-519), but there's NO visual indication this is possible. Users don't know they can swipe.

3. **No Tap-to-Select Phage**: The phage name appears in the header but isn't tappable. Users must use Prev/Next buttons or swipe to navigate, with no quick-select dropdown.

## User Impact

- **Newcomers** don't discover swipe navigation exists
- **Everyone** can't quickly jump to a specific phage on mobile
- **Visual confusion** from overlapping UI elements damages perceived quality

## Design Philosophy

Mobile-first, gesture-first navigation following iOS/Android conventions:
- **Gesture hints** that teach and then get out of the way
- **Tap affordances** on interactive elements (phage name should look tappable)
- **Progressive disclosure**: hints for newcomers, clean UI for returning users

## Technical Context

Relevant files:
- `packages/web/src/styles/fab.css` - FAB positioning (z-index: --z-fixed)
- `packages/web/src/styles/control-deck.css` - Bottom nav bar (z-index: --z-fixed)
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx:440-519` - Swipe gesture handling
- `packages/web/src/components/mobile/ControlDeck.tsx` - Bottom navigation
- `packages/web/src/styles/mobile-ux.css` - .gesture-hint class exists but unused
- `packages/web/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` - Header with phage name

## Success Criteria

- FAB no longer occludes any navigation indicators
- First-time mobile users understand they can swipe
- Phage name in header is tappable with dropdown picker
- Swipe indicators auto-dismiss after learned
- Mobile navigation feels native-quality (iOS/Android level polish)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T17:34:50.915175075Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:54:42.819363501Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:54:42.819363501Z","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: FAB repositioning, swipe indicators, hint persistence, and phage picker dropdown","compaction_level":0}

Build and typecheck pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-35ce","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-wzjl","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:14:11.765986147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-39gi","title":"Web: SequenceView scroll black flash + renderer stability","description":"## User-facing problem
On mobile, the Sequence viewer (canvas) shows a **black/blank region** while scrolling; the newly revealed area appears black before the bases render. This looks broken and makes the app feel janky/unpolished.

## Why this matters
The sequence viewer is one of the highest-traffic surfaces. Any scroll flicker/blanking immediately reads as “the app can’t keep up” and damages the premium feel.

## Likely root causes (high-signal suspects)
1) **Post-process tracking bug**: `CanvasSequenceGridRenderer.render()` tracks `lastFrameHadPostProcess` based on the *presence* of a post-process pipeline instead of whether processing actually occurred. If the CRT/WebGL pipeline is unavailable or returns false (unsupported / disabled / error), we still treat the last frame as post-processed, which can unnecessarily disable incremental scroll blits and contribute to visible blanking/jank.
2) **Scroll blit + post-process interaction**: incremental blit is intentionally disabled when the previous frame truly used CRT/WebGL post-processing; we must track whether post-processing actually ran (vs. falling back).
3) **Mobile resize/viewport churn**: iOS can resize canvases during scroll (URL bar / visual viewport changes). Canvases created with `{ alpha: false }` clear to opaque black on resize unless we explicitly paint the background immediately.

## Goals
- Eliminate any runtime errors in the sequence renderer (no console errors, no render loop aborts).
- Ensure the sequence canvas **never flashes to default black** during scroll/resize; background should match theme surface.
- Keep incremental scroll-blit fast path working whenever safe, so scroll feels native.

## Proposed implementation
- In `CanvasSequenceGridRenderer.render()`, track whether post-processing actually occurred (e.g., `didApplyPostProcess`) using the boolean return value from `PostProcessPipeline.process()`.
- Ensure both the back-buffer and non-back-buffer paths record whether post-processing ran.
- Keep existing background fill behavior on resize + during render as defense-in-depth.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Scrolling the sequence on iOS Safari / Chrome Android shows no black/blank tiles; newly revealed rows render smoothly.
- [ ] No render-loop exceptions during sequence interaction.
- [ ] CRT/WebGL post-process still works when enabled; incremental scroll blit remains correct.

## Test plan
- Unit: add a small renderer smoke test in `packages/web` that mounts a canvas in a DOM-like test env (or minimal DOM shim), runs one render tick, and asserts no throw.
- E2E (later / separate bead if needed): Playwright mobile emulation scrolls the sequence container and asserts the canvas is painted (non-zero pixel variance) before/after scroll.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-13T07:08:34.959273606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T07:38:29.457519848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T07:38:29.457519848Z","close_reason":"Fixed: track actual post-process application so scroll-blit isn’t unnecessarily disabled; improves mobile scroll blanking","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-39gi","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-13T07:08:34.964660490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-3cl","title":"Advanced: CRISPR Pressure & Anti-CRISPR Landscape","description":"Integrate host CRISPR spacer hits + Acr predictions (HMMs, small ORF heuristics, ESM embedding similarity). Pressure score combining spacer density, PAM proximity, coding strand. Pressure bar with spacer ticks, Acr candidates glow; center on strongest clash. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## CRISPR PRESSURE & ANTI-CRISPR LANDSCAPE

**Full TypeScript Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Visualize the evolutionary arms race between CRISPR targeting and phage counter-defenses with pressure mapping, Acr prediction, and escape mutation analysis.

### Mathematical Foundations

**CRISPR Pressure Index:**
```
P(window) = Σ_spacers [ match_score × PAM_score × strand_weight ]

- match_score: Sequence identity (0-1)
- PAM_score: 1.0 if valid PAM, 0.3 otherwise
- strand_weight: 1.0 coding, 0.7 template
```

**Acr Candidate Score:**
```
Acr_score = w1 × HMM_hit + w2 × size_fit + w3 × neighborhood + w4 × disorder

- HMM_hit: Match to AcrIIA, AcrIIC, AcrIF families
- size_fit: Optimal 50-200 aa range
- neighborhood: Proximity to spacer hotspots
- disorder: Charged residue fraction
```

**Escape Mutation Priority:**
```
E(position) = CRISPR_coverage × (1 - conservation) × synonymous_option
```

### Complete TypeScript Implementation

- `SpacerHit` (host, crisprType I/II/III/V/VI, match, PAM, strand)
- `AcrCandidate` (gene, score, family, disorder, confidence)
- `CRISPRPressureWindow` (pressure index, spacer count, dominant type)
- `EscapeMutation` (position, suggested base, affected spacers)
- `PAM_PATTERNS` for Cas9, Cas12, Cas13
- `ACR_HMM_PATTERNS` for AcrIIA, AcrIIC, AcrIF
- `checkPAM()` PAM recognition by CRISPR type
- `predictAcrScore()` combining HMM, size, disorder, proximity
- `computePressureWindows()` sliding window analysis
- `analyzeCRISPRPressure()` main pipeline
- `renderCRISPRPressureBar()` ASCII gradient with Acr markers

### TUI Features
- Pressure map: ░ low → █ high pressure
- Spacer hit markers (|)
- Acr candidate stars (★)
- Hotspot detail panel with spacer table
- Host organism, CRISPR type, match %, PAM status
- Acr candidate table with family, score, confidence
- Arms race interpretation
- [←/→] Navigate hotspots, [A] View Acr, [E] Escape mutations

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-07T05:18:13.421330Z","updated_at":"2025-12-07T22:15:53.673192Z","closed_at":"2025-12-07T22:15:53.673192Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-3cl","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T05:20:54.384769Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
